title: Understanding contracts
description: Practice understanding of contracts: contract components and implications.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ivan signed an employment contract to work at Widget Co. The contract had a non-compete clause. A few years later, he resigned and started working for Gadget Co, a competitor.

**What is the most likely outcome of this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Ivan could get sued by Widget Co. for breaking a contract.
Ivan will have to return to Widget Co.
Ivan will be fired by Gadget Co.
There will be no consequence because Ivan resigned before going to work for a competitor.

assessment_items.question_content:
John recently adopted a dog, assuming pets were allowed in his apartment building because he saw other tenants with them. However, his landlord has informed him that keeping pets is a clear violation of the agreement in his lease.

**What is the most likely outcome of this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: John will be given a special exemption to keep his dog because he was unaware of the pet policy.
John could face eviction if he does not find a new home for his dog.
All tenants with dogs will be evicted.
John can keep the dog because other tenants have dogs, too.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sarah has leased vehicles in the past and assumed the mileage allowed in her current lease would be similar to previous ones. She now realizes that she exceeded the mileage limit specified in the current contract. After returning the car, the dealership informed her that she owes $\$2{,}000$ due to the excess mileage.

**What will most likely happen in this situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The dealership will give Sarah a one-time  special exemption because she has leased before.
The dealer will have to work out a payment plan with Sara to pay off the miles.
She will need to pay the additional $\$2{,}000$ for exceeding the mileage limit.
Nothing will happen, as she has already returned the car.

assessment_items.question_content:
When signing the loan paperwork, James did not notice that his home loan had an adjustable interest rate. After two years, James noticed that his monthly payments increased by several hundred dollars. He contacted the bank, and they informed him that the interest rate had adjusted according to the terms of the loan.

**What is most likely to happen in this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: James can immediately go back to his original monthly payment amount by requesting a fixed rate.
James will continue making the higher monthly payments as per his contract.
The bank will have to lower his interest rate back to the original level since James was unaware of the change.
James can refuse to pay the increased amount until the bank adjusts the loan terms.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ariana uses her car for a ride-share job and recently got into a car accident. Her insurance company denied her claim, citing an [[☃ definition 1]] in the policy. The policy does not cover accidents that occur while using the car for ride-sharing services.

**What is the likely outcome of  this scenario?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: a situation or event for which the insurance company will not pay benefits or provide coverage.

choices.content: Ariana will get the insurance company to cover the damages because she will sue  them for breaking the contract.
Ariana will be responsible for paying the repair costs herself because her insurance policy excludes coverage for ride-sharing activities.
Ariana will get the damages paid by having the exclusion removed from the contract.
Ariana can switch insurance companies to get the damages paid by the new insurance.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lisa took a trip abroad and assumed her cell phone plan included international [[☃ definition 1]] at no extra cost. When she returned home, her bill had hundreds of dollars in roaming charges. She contacted her service provider, and they informed her that her plan did not include free international roaming.

**Which of the following is the best option for handling these extra charges?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: using your phone on a different network, often when traveling. It can lead to extra charges for calls, texts, and data.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lydia lives in a condominium that is part of a Homeowners' Association (HOA). The HOA covenants require her to pay a monthly fee, which pays for landscaping around her property.

**What is the *consideration* in this contract?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Contracts can go by various names depending on their purpose and context.

**Which of the following is another term for "contract"?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You found a car that you like. You drove it, liked it, and made an offer that the seller accepted. You shook hands and you left to get a cashier’s check from the bank so you could pay for the car.    

**What part of the contract is demonstrated when you and the seller discuss and finalize the car sale by shaking hands?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Your neighbor asked for help clearing up debris after a strong storm, and offered you $\$20$ for your help. You agreed and shook hands with the neighbor. After you were done, the neighbor handed you the $\$20$.

**What part of a contract does the payment of $\$20$ represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Andrea wants to sell her car to Sergio. Andrea is $18$ years old and Sergio is $17$ years old.

**Which part of the contract might be problematic because of Sergio’s age?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Rey lives in a county where fireworks are not allowed due to wildfire risk. He placed an order for six boxes of fireworks for his 50th birthday celebration and paid online. The seller never delivered the fireworks and Rey sued, but the court denied the case, saying it was invalid.

**Which part of the contract was missing to make this contract valid?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
